articles.title: SpaceX Makes History With World's Biggest Ever Stock Market Launch
articles.summary: Space company SpaceX officially began trading on the American stock market on June 12, 2026, in what is being called the largest initial public offering (IPO) in history. Each share was priced at US$135, and the company raised a record-breaking US$75 billion — more than double the previous world record held by Saudi Aramco in 2019. Ordinary investors were so excited about the launch that they put in orders worth over US$100 billion, far more than the shares available to them. SpaceX is the rocket company founded by Elon Musk, known for sending astronauts to space and launching thousands of satellites.

lv1: What is the name of the space company that started selling its shares on the stock market today? (今天開始在股票市場出售股票的太空公司叫什麼名字？)
How much money did SpaceX raise when it listed on the stock market? (SpaceX上市時籌集了多少錢？)
lv2: Why do you think so many ordinary people wanted to buy shares in SpaceX? (你認為為什麼這麼多普通人想購買SpaceX的股票？)
What does it mean for a company to be listed on the stock market, and how might it help SpaceX grow? (一家公司在股票市場上市是什麼意思，這對SpaceX的發展有什麼幫助？)
lv3: Should ordinary people be encouraged to invest in high-risk companies like space businesses? What are the benefits and dangers? (應否鼓勵普通人投資像太空公司這類高風險企業？有什麼好處和危險？)

When one person like Elon Musk controls both a major space company and other powerful businesses, is that good or bad for society? Give reasons for both sides. (當一個人如埃隆·馬斯克同時控制一家重要太空公司及其他強大企業時，對社會是好是壞？請列舉兩方理由。)

articles.title: Hong Kong Tries Out New 'Hands-Free' Border Crossing Technology at HZMB
articles.summary: Hong Kong's Immigration Department is introducing a brand-new type of e-channel at the border that works without passengers needing to stop and scan anything. This 'contactless' system will be tested first at the Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macao Bridge (HZMB) departure hall, starting on June 25, 2026. The technology uses sensors to automatically recognise travellers as they walk through, making crossing the border faster and more convenient. If successful, this smart system could be expanded to other border checkpoints across Hong Kong in the future, helping to reduce long queues for travellers.

lv1: Where will Hong Kong's new contactless e-channel be tested first? (香港新的無感應式電子通道將首先在哪裡測試？)
On what date will the new e-channel system begin operating? (新電子通道系統將於哪天開始運作？)
lv2: How might this new 'hands-free' border technology make travelling easier for people crossing the HZMB? (這種新的「無感應」邊境技術如何讓過港珠澳大橋的旅客出行更方便？)
What kinds of technology might the new system use to recognise travellers without them stopping? (新系統可能使用哪些技術來識別旅客而不需要他們停下來？)
lv3: Smart border technology collects personal data such as facial or movement information. Do you think this is a fair trade-off for faster travel? Why or why not? (智能邊境技術會收集面部或行動等個人資料。你認為以此換取更快通關是否公平？為什麼？)
If contactless border technology spreads to all checkpoints, what could be the positive and negative effects on Hong Kong's society and privacy? (如果無感應式邊境技術推廣至所有關口，對香港社會和私隱會有什麼正面和負面影響？)

articles.title: 香港公務員獲加薪2%　工會指中低層加幅創十年新低
articles.summary: 香港政府宣布，公務員薪酬將整體上調2%。然而，有工會批評，這個加幅對中低層公務員來說是近十年最低的一次，未能反映他們的實際生活負擔。工會亦擔心，若日後引入更多不確定因素來決定薪酬調整，情況可能更難以預料。政府方面則表示，加薪幅度已考慮到未來的財務承擔能力。公務員薪酬問題影響本港數萬名打工族，引起社會廣泛關注。

lv1: 香港政府宣布公務員加薪多少百分比？
哪個群體的工會對今次加薪幅度表示不滿？
lv2: 為什麼工會認為2%的加薪幅度對中低層公務員不公平？
政府在決定公務員薪酬時，需要考慮哪些不同因素？
lv3: 政府應該優先考慮控制財政開支，還是應該確保公務員獲得足夠的薪酬？請從兩個角度分析。
如果公務員薪酬長期偏低，可能對香港的公共服務質素帶來什麼影響？

articles.title: 「在校課後託管」幫助逾萬小學生　校園變成「第二個家」
articles.summary: 全港207間小學今學年參加「在校課後託管服務計劃」，提供約10,500個名額。學生放學後可以留在學校，由專人指導完成功課，還可以閱讀和下棋。政府希望學生回家前先做完功課，增加親子相處時間，令家庭關係更和諧。有機構表示，託管服務不只是功課輔導，更像為學生打造校園裏的「第二個家」。

lv1: 放學後，參加託管計劃的同學可以在學校做甚麼？
如果你可以留在學校參加課後活動，你最想做甚麼？為甚麼？
lv2: 為甚麼在學校完成功課後才回家，可以增加親子相處時間？
你認為學校像「第二個家」嗎？試說出一個原因。
lv3: 有人認為託管服務只是功課輔導班，也有人認為它同時照顧學童的情緒需要。你比較同意哪一個看法？為甚麼？
如果你是校長，你會怎樣安排課後託管活動，令不同需要的同學都受惠？
